mechanism_narrative: A persistent expansion in real GDP growth compresses equity risk premiums by confirming corporate earnings resilience, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in monthly employment growth forces market repricing of recession probabilities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 50.0 thousand
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous federal funds rate hikes and declining real estate construction activity signal aggressive monetary tightening colliding with weakening demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with Rank IC greater than 0.04 over a 3-year rolling window, or single-proxy models consistently outperform the interaction term.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth above trend validates capacity expansion narratives, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days specifically during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain flat or negative when INDPRO exceeds 100, or the hypothesis loses predictive power during high-inflation environments.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in consumer credit outstanding triggers immediate risk-off positioning as liquidity constraints tighten, depressing S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month change drops below -5000.0 million
failure_condition: Credit contractions coincide with positive SPX returns in more than 50 percent of observations, or the signal shows no incremental predictive power over volatility indices.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment coinciding with elevated federal funds rates confirms a stagflationary regime, forcing discount rate expansion and earnings downgrades that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises greater than 0.3pp in 3 months
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous growth in industrial output reduces supply chain bottlenecks and input cost inflation, supporting margin expansion and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant difference between high and low INDPRO growth regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating core inflation readings during a late-cycle expansion force premature rate hike expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Inflation spikes consistently trigger positive SPX returns due to nominal earnings adjustments, or the signal fails to predict returns when FEDFUNDS remains below 2.0 percent.
